Ahvazi, Niusha, Andrew B. Pace, Christopher T. Garling, et al. 2025. “The abundance and properties of the lowest luminosity dwarf galaxies around the Milky Way: Insights from Semi-Analytic Models.” arXiv e-Prints, November, arXiv:2511.15808. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2511.15808.


Bhowmick, Aklant K., Laura Blecha, Paul Torrey, et al. 2025. “Signatures of black hole seeding in the local Universe: predictions from the BRAHMA cosmological simulations.” 538 (1): 518–36. https://doi.org/10.1093/mnras/staf269.


Bhowmick, Aklant K., Laura Blecha, Paul Torrey, et al. 2026. “Heavy Seeds and the First Black Holes: Insights from the BRAHMA Simulations.” 997 (2): 187. https://doi.org/10.3847/1538-4357/ae2607.


Garcia, Alex M., Jonah C. Rose, Paul Torrey, et al. 2026. “The DREAMS Project: Disentangling the Impact of Halo-to-halo Variance and Baryonic Feedback on Milky Way Dark Matter Density Profiles.” 1002 (1): 8. https://doi.org/10.3847/1538-4357/ae5938.


Kho, Jonathan, Aklant Kumar Bhowmick, Paul Torrey, et al. 2025. “Signatures of Black Hole Seeding on the M\(_{\bullet}\)─\(\sigma\) Relation: Predictions from the BRAHMA Simulations.” 994 (2): 172. https://doi.org/10.3847/1538-4357/ae0e1e.


Kho, Jonathan, Aklant K. Bhowmick, Rainer Weinberger, et al. 2026. “Learning the Universe at High Redshifts: Impact of Accretion Modeling on Early Black Hole Growth.” arXiv e-Prints, June, arXiv:2606.10036. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2606.10036.


Kho, Jonathan, and Shazrene Mohamed. 2024. “3D Moving-Mesh Simulations of Type IIn Supernovae.” American Astronomical Society Meeting Abstracts #243, American astronomical society meeting abstracts, vol. 243 (February): 260.14.

title: Jonathan Kho
text: I’m a PhD student at the University of Virginia studying supermassive black holes and galaxies in cosmological simulations
